name: Heksh
bio: Heksh is the grand monk of the Shin-Ju and he wants complete dominion of the fighting world, which he states without preamble or justification because he does not consider it to require either. He is an unstoppable force with one goal and no interest in goals that aren't that one, which makes him simple in the way that the most dangerous things tend to be simple — no competing priorities, no doubt, nothing to work with except the fact of him.

He used the tournament as a mechanism to acquire the talisman, which he had identified as the thing that would take him from very difficult to defeat to effectively unbeatable. The talisman amplified what was already there, which was already enormous. He stepped onto the mat before the scheduled final could begin and dismissed the remaining structure because the structure had served its purpose.

He was beaten by everyone going at him together, which is the only way something like Heksh gets beaten — not by one person's individual greatness but by the specific aggregate of everyone Montana had encountered, made, or stumbled into across five days in Texas. Montana's white Ki found the opening. The rest of them made the opening possible. This is, when you think about it, a reasonable summary of what the story was about.
